title: age-group — validation rules
description: Five custom Laravel validation rules published by AgeGroupServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate AgeGroup-adjacent fields. Named after their intent so they read cleanly at the composition site.

rules.name: valid_hex_color
rules.description: Ensures the value is a well-formed CSS hex color literal — either 3-digit shorthand (#RGB) or 6-digit full (#RRGGBB). Case-insensitive. Rejects the 8-digit ARGB form (alpha channel is out of scope for age-group visualisation).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid hex color (#RGB or #RRGGBB).

rules.name: valid_age_bounds
rules.description: Cross-field validator. Applied on the data object (not on an individual field). Ensures min <= max, min >= 3 (platform floor), max <= 100 (platform ceiling), and both are integers. Runs BEFORE the observer's redundant guard so callers see the clean 422 error before hitting the write path.

params.name: min_field
params.description: Field name on the data object holding the min age.

params.name: max_field
params.description: Field name on the data object holding the max age.

rules.default_message: The age bounds must satisfy: 3 <= min_age <= max_age <= 100.

rules.name: valid_cutoff_date
rules.description: Cross-field validator. Ensures the (cutoff_date_kind, cutoff_month, cutoff_day) triple is internally consistent. calendar_year requires both month + day to be NULL. academic_year requires month (1-12) and allows day to be NULL (defaults to 1). custom_date requires both month (1-12) and day (1-31) — with month-appropriate day validation (Feb ≤ 29, 30-day months ≤ 30). rolling_from_birthday requires both to be NULL.
rules.default_message: The cutoff date configuration is inconsistent for the selected kind.

rules.name: valid_sport_key
rules.description: Ensures the value matches the sport-key syntax `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$` (lowercase snake, starts with a letter). In Wave 3c the rule hardens: with `registry` mode set, it also checks the value against the sport registry (a shipped table of known sport identifiers). Until then, `registry` mode is a no-op — every syntactically-valid value passes.

params.name: mode
params.description: syntax = only regex check; registry = also verify against the shipped sports registry (Wave 3c).

rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid sport identifier (lowercase, snake_case, starts with a letter).

rules.name: age_group_slug_reserved
rules.description: Rejects slugs that collide with platform-reserved values (default, system, seeded, admin, platform). Reserved slugs are used by the seeder + admin tooling; tenants must pick a different slug. Case-insensitive comparison.
rules.default_message: The slug ':input' is reserved. Please choose a different slug.
